1.Title: Instability in Response Time
1.Insight: Despite stable virtual users for Homepage, the response time fluctuated significantly, indicating possible backend processing variability or intermittent bottlenecks.
1.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained at **10** from 11:08 AM UTC to 11:10 AM UTC. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) for Homepage dropped from 3590ms at 11:08 AM UTC to 2220ms at 11:08 AM UTC, then oscillated between 1920ms and 2430ms during the rest of the test (until 11:10 AM UTC), with no stabilization. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated as well, peaking at 6.4 RPS at 11:09 AM UTC and ending at 2.9 RPS at 11:10 AM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider profiling backend services to identify any resource contention, queuing delays, or variable data access times. Evaluate application logs for transient errors or slow requests. You may also investigate potential thread pool exhaustion or transient dependency latency spikes.

2.Title: RPS Decline Despite Active Users
2.Insight: Requests per second for Homepage decreased by over **50%** towards test end, despite nearly constant virtual users, suggesting server back-pressure or throttling.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) peaked at **6.4 RPS at 11:09 AM UTC** and gradually dropped to 2.9 RPS at 11:10 AM UTC for Homepage. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) stayed at **10** until 11:10 AM UTC, only dropping to 8 at 11:10 AM UTC. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) remained high and variable throughout.
2.Recommendation: Consider reviewing server logs for signs of throttling or rate limiting. Assess whether application or infrastructure scaling is adequate to maintain throughput. You may also check for dependency slowdowns or resource exhaustion impacts.

3.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) ran successfully with minor latency spikes. Response times fluctuated between 1.9–2.4s and throughput varied, suggesting periodic backend constraints.

1.Title: Fluctuating Response Time Under Steady Load
1.Insight: For Homepage, response time showed **persistent oscillations** between 1920ms and 2430ms while [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained steady, indicating inconsistent server performance despite stable simulated traffic.
1.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) initially decreased from 3590ms at 11:08 AM UTC to 2220ms at 11:08 AM UTC, then alternated between **1920ms and 2430ms** from 11:08 AM UTC to 11:10 AM UTC. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) held constant at 10 from 11:08 AM UTC to 11:10 AM UTC, suggesting the server should have had consistent processing load. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) remained within 4.6 to 6.4 RPS during this period.
1.Recommendation: Consider profiling the server for thread starvation, intermittent resource contention (such as DB or cache bottlenecks), or balancing issues. Review server logs for time intervals with high response time spikes.

2.Title: RPS Drop at End of Test
2.Insight: Requests per second for [ALT-Sampler-4dd9ff6d-477e-43dc-b4bb-111238174586](#ResourceLink) fell from **6.4 to 2.9 RPS** at the end of the test, coinciding with a reduction in [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) from 10 to 8. This may indicate either test ramp-down or potential client resource constraints.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 6.4 at 11:10 AM UTC to **2.9 at 11:10 AM UTC**. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) decreased from 10 at 11:10 AM UTC to 8 at 11:10 AM UTC. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) persisted in the same fluctuating range despite user decrease.
2.Recommendation: If this drop was not intentional, consider reviewing the load test client CPU and memory usage, as well as test configuration for user ramp-down triggers. If test intended, ignore; otherwise, validate engine health near end of test.

3.Title: Response Time Decrease After Initial Spike
3.Insight: Initial response time for [ALT-Sampler-4dd9ff6d-477e-43dc-b4bb-111238174586](#ResourceLink) started high at **3590ms**, then decreased and stabilized, indicating either cold start delays or initial server ramp-up effects.
3.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) was **3590ms at 11:08 AM UTC**, dropped to 2220ms at 11:08 AM UTC, then oscillated between 1920ms and 2430ms for the remainder of the run. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) was constant at 10 during this phase.
3.Recommendation: Review application cold start behavior, initialization routines, or early resource allocation. Consider adding warm-up steps before starting measurement phase in future tests to smooth out startup latency.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) ran successfully with occasional latency fluctuations. Response times remained elevated with minor oscillations, and throughput showed slight drops towards the end, indicating potential backend constraints.
